---
title: "Starter Analysis"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

## Purpose

This file is the main starting point for the repository.
It shows a simple Quarto workflow with text, code, output, and a graph.

## Load Packages

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggthemes)
```

## Load the Data

```{r}
course_data <- read_csv("data/example_data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

course_data |>
  slice_head(n = 6)
```

## Inspect the Variables

```{r}
glimpse(course_data)
```

## Summary Table

```{r}
course_data |>
  group_by(section) |>
  summarise(
    mean_study_hours = mean(study_hours, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_exam_score = mean(exam_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_project_score = mean(project_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

## First Plot

```{r}
course_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = study_hours, y = exam_score, color = section)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  ggthemes::theme_clean() +
  labs(
    x = "Study hours",
    y = "Exam score",
    color = "Section"
  )
```

## Next Step

Edit one sentence in this file, save it, run `quarto render starter_analysis.qmd`, and then open `starter_analysis.html`.
